message:
feat(mvcc): MICA partitioned commit log primitive (IMPL-24 / AG-5C)

MT-bench shows fsqlite throughput degrading from 4 to 8 concurrent writer
threads because every committer serializes on a single queue lock. MICA
(Lim et al., NSDI 2014) sidesteps that by partitioning per-thread state
so the hot path never touches a cross-core mutex.

This lands a standalone MVP of the MICA commit-log idea in
`fsqlite-mvcc::mica_commit_log`:

* `MicaShard { entries: Mutex<Vec<CommitEntry>>, shard_seq: AtomicU64 }`
  — one shard per partition; mutex hold time bounded to a single
  `Vec::push` so contention scales with appenders-per-shard rather
  than total threads.
* `CommitEntry { commit_seq, page_numbers, timestamp_ns }` carries its
  own global sequence number so the reader can reconstruct a totally
  ordered stream regardless of which shard accepted the append.
* `MicaCommitLog::new(num_shards)` — one shard per CPU core at the call
  site; `num_shards == 0` is defensively clamped to 1.
* `append(commit_seq, page_numbers)` — shard chosen by hashing
  `thread::current().id()` with xxh3 plus a SplitMix64 finalizer to
  guard against low-entropy consecutive ThreadId bit patterns; no
  `unsafe` and no `libc::sched_getcpu`.
* `merge_ordered()` — O(N log S) k-way merge backed by `BinaryHeap`
  over (commit_seq, shard_idx, pos) triples. Ties on `commit_seq` are
  broken by shard index so the output is deterministic across runs.
* `shard_count()`, `total_len()`, `is_empty()` accessors for
  diagnostics.

Tests (all passing, 5 total):
1. Single thread, 100 sequential appends → merge_ordered returns them
   in commit_seq order with strictly increasing sequence.
2. Four threads × 100 disjoint-range appends → merge_ordered returns
   all 400 entries in commit_seq order.
3. Empty log → merge_ordered returns an empty Vec.
4. `num_shards == 0` is clamped to 1 and the log still works.
5. Ties on commit_seq yield a stable, repeatable ordering.

Integration is intentionally minimal: `pub mod mica_commit_log;` is
added to `fsqlite-mvcc/src/lib.rs` but no real commit path is rewired.
That is deferred to the follow-up bead which will compose this with
`core_types::CommitLog` and `write_coordinator::WriteCoordinator`.

Verified via rch: `cargo check -p fsqlite-mvcc` clean,
`cargo clippy -p fsqlite-mvcc --lib --tests -- -D warnings` clean,
`cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc --lib mica_commit_log` — 5 passed, 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
